summary: Eight concrete items surfaced from today's HN front page.
telegram_footer: 8 items surfaced | 24/24 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: ZCode
items.source_story_title: ZCode: Claude Code from the Makers of GLM
items.summary: A Claude Code-style desktop app from the GLM team.
items.why_included: HN commenters compared it to Mimo Code and discussed SSH, Docker, and VM workflows.
items.evidence: Replies focused on headless-versus-desktop use, remote hosts, and whether telemetry or open source should matter.

items.name: Parsewise
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Parsewise (YC P25) – Reason Across Documents with an API
items.summary: An API for reasoning across document buckets with traceable lineage.
items.why_included: Commenters treated validation and provenance as the product’s core value.
items.evidence: Replies compared it to Struktur and asked how it handles endless file-type combinations and custom instructions.

items.name: Z-Jail
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Z-Jail – A 130 KB Linux sandbox-C99 with 7 defense layers and zero deps
items.summary: A 130 KB Linux sandbox for CI and code evaluation.
items.why_included: Commenters zeroed in on its syscall allowlist and security use cases.
items.evidence: Replies debated how strict it is, whether security code can be readable, and whether CI runners need such tight isolation.

items.name: Box3D
items.source_story_title: Box3D, an open source 3D physics engine
items.summary: An open-source 3D physics engine from the Box2D author.
items.why_included: HN immediately treated it as a serious sibling to Box2D.
items.evidence: Replies linked the GitHub repo and talked about Erin Catto, Angry Birds physics, and the library’s pedigree.

items.name: Monetization Gateway
items.summary: Cloudflare’s payment gate for AI agents and web traffic.
items.why_included: The thread quickly centered on x402, spam economics, and centralization risk.
items.evidence: Commenters debated whether this becomes a web payment layer or a new bottleneck for bots, privacy, and publishers.

items.name: Simple Made Easy
items.source_story_title: What to Learn to Be a Graphics Programmer
items.summary: Rich Hickey’s talk on simplicity versus ease.
items.why_included: A commenter singled it out as a classic that still shapes design choices.
items.evidence: The thread explicitly linked the talk and described it as still influencing how one commenter designs software.

items.name: Essential Mathematics for Games and Interactive Applications
items.source_story_title: What to Learn to Be a Graphics Programmer
items.summary: A game-programming math book for graphics and simulation.
items.why_included: Graphics-programming commenters recommended it as the math foundation to learn next.
items.evidence: Replies pointed to it after discussing SDL3 GPU, Odin, and other graphics learning resources.

items.name: Children of Time
items.source_story_title: Newly discovered spider builds spring loaded snare to catch ants
items.summary: Adrian Tchaikovsky’s science-fiction novel about evolving spiders.
items.why_included: The spider thread turned into a strong book recommendation pile.
items.evidence: Multiple commenters said 'Children of Time' is fantastic, seconded it, and ranked it among their best reads.
